overview: A four-color ramp strategy that leverages mana dorks like Formidable Speaker and Ashling, Rekindled to accelerate into game-winning spells. Omniscience serves as the primary engine, allowing you to cast multiple threats or answers without paying mana. The deck aims to outvalue opponents by generating massive mana advantages and closing games with efficient, untapped threats.
win_conditions: Casting multiple threats for free via Omniscience

keep_criteria: Hands with early mana dorks and land drops
Hands containing Omniscience with follow-up spells
Hands with interactive removal or ramp
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (duals, fetches, or ramp like Sol Ring/Arcane Signet) plus at least one early spell engine (Kona, Formidable Speaker, or a cheap cantrip/ramp).
Prioritize keeping hands that can cast 2+ spells by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking ramp or mana acceleration
Hands with too many lands and no threats
Hands missing Omniscience or key finishers
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, only lands, or a hand of high-cost finishers without early outlets.

early_game: Play lands and mana dorks on curve
Establish early board presence with Speaker
Prioritize land drops over early spells
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a mana accelerator or cantrip.
Turn 2: Ramp into Kona or Formidable Speaker, or cast a cheap Elemental to start the token pipeline.
Sequence lands to ensure 3-4 mana by turn 3.
If Omniscience is in hand, hold it until you can cast a high-value spell for free, then drop it to swing for lethal or establish board dominance.
mid_game: Cast Omniscience when you have multiple spells ready
Follow up with threats and answers simultaneously
Use Ashling to generate extra mana or untap lands
Treat mana as a resource for spell density, not just for casting Kona.
Keep 1-2 lands in hand if drawing multiple spells.
Avoid self-mill unless it fuels a specific win condition.
Use cantrips to maintain hand size and continuously trigger Kona/Formidable Speaker.
Commit threats when you have removal or counterspells to protect them, or when you can out-value the opponent's board.
Hold back if facing heavy board wipes or targeted removal
late_game: Flood the board with untapped creatures
Close out games with efficient, high-impact threats
Maintain mana advantage to outvalue opponents
Omniscience + Ashling, Rekindled or Kona/Formidable Speaker is the primary win condition.
Cast Omniscience, then cast Ashling or multiple Elementals for free to flood the board with flying tokens.
Alternatively, use a high-impact spell or big Elemental finisher to close out the game.
Go all-in when you have Omniscience in play, enough mana to cast multiple spells, and the opponent has no immediate board wipe or removal.
Also commit when you can out-value their remaining life total with token damage and have no reason to hold back interaction.

key_cards.name: Formidable Speaker
key_cards.role: Mana dork and early board presence
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to accelerate into turn 3-4 spells; can block or attack to apply pressure.

key_cards.name: Omniscience
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have multiple spells ready; prioritize threats followed by answers or more ramp.

key_cards.name: Ashling, Rekindled
key_cards.role: Secondary mana dork and card advantage
key_cards.notes: Use to untap lands or generate extra mana; synergizes with Omniscience for massive turns.

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for counterspells if needed, but prioritize casting Omniscience when you have a clear follow-up
Play mana dorks before casting Omniscience to maximize free spells
Save removal for opponent's key threats
Kona and Formidable Speaker trigger on any spell you cast, including instants and activated abilities that count as spells.
Cast instants at optimal times to maximize token generation.
protect it with counterspells or sacrifice outlets if needed.
Common mistake: overcommitting to Kona without follow-up spells.
Always keep a hand of spells to fuel the engine.

matchups.advice: Play dorks early, use Omniscience to cast multiple blockers or a big threat
Ramp past their early game, use Omniscience to cast threats and answers simultaneously
Outvalue them with mana advantage, close quickly with untapped threats

Use cheap, multi-target spells or cast instants in response to their counters to trigger Kona/Formidable Speaker. Use spell acceleration to cast multiple spells in a single turn, forcing them to counter multiple targets. Prioritize uncounterable effects or spell-skew if available.

Apply early pressure with cheap Elementals and cantrips. Use removal to hit their ramp spells or mana rocks. Do not let them establish a massive mana advantage; disrupt their early game with interaction and keep the board state fluid.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card draw, removal, and counterspells. Control the board with Kona/Formidable Speaker, but be ready to trade efficiently. Use hand disruption to break their rhythm and win by out-resourcing and out-answering.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (Cyclonic Rift, Wrath of God), targeted removal (Path to Exile, Lightning Strike), and counterspells.
Note when opponents are holding up mana for interaction vs. casting spells.
Track their graveyard if they are playing combo or reanimation strategies.
